diagrams.title: kill() Function Parameters
diagrams.description: Shows kill() function with two parameters: pid (process identifier) and sig (signal number)

diagrams.title: Signal Flow: kill() vs raise()
diagrams.description: Comparative diagram showing how kill() sends signals to other processes while raise() sends to itself
elements: Process A calling kill(PID_B, SIGTERM) → Process B receives signal
Process A calling raise(SIGTERM) → Process A receives its own signal
Visual: Arrow from A to B (kill), Arrow from A to itself (raise)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing the difference between inter-process and self signals

diagrams.title: Signal Values and Actions
diagrams.description: Common Unix signals with their numbers, names, and default actions
diagrams.purpose: Memorizing important signal numbers and their effects

diagrams.title: kill() pid Parameter Variations
diagrams.description: Different behaviors based on pid value in kill() function
elements: pid = -1: Send to all accessible processes
diagrams.purpose: Understanding pid parameter behavior in kill()

visuals.title: Kill And Raise Functions
visuals.description: Visual diagram for kill and raise functions
